title: Reflecting on the riots at the US Capitol, plus: Georgia runoff elections, vaccine distribution & more

Show Notes:

0:00 New intro for the besties - listen here: https://rb.gy/aizgdm

2:14 Sacks' trip to Miami

6:01 Reflecting on the riot at the US Capitol: police response, double standard with BLM protest, big picture, prosecuting Trump & healing the nation post-Trump

29:43 2016 Election interference, reasons for unrest & polarization, Trump's culpability

44:19 Should the 25th Amendment be invoked?

49:51 Democrats win Georgia runoff elections, did Trump's implosion lose Georgia for the GOP?

56:23 How Friedberg would handle vaccine distribution

1:07:45 San Francisco's Killer D.A., recalling Gavin Newsom, Kim Kardashian for Governor of CA

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses the US Capitol riot, Georgia runoff elections, vaccine distribution, and more. They reflect on the police response, double standard with BLM protests, and Trump's culpability. The hosts also discuss the 25th Amendment and Trump's impact on the GOP.

takeaways.text: Police response to US Capitol riot was inadequate
Double standard exists between US Capitol riot and BLM protests
Trump's actions contributed to unrest and polarization
Trump's implosion may have lost Georgia for the GOP
Vaccine distribution requires a data-driven approach
Recall efforts against Gavin Newsom are underway
Kim Kardashian's potential gubernatorial run was discussed

sentiment.notes: The hosts expressed concern about the US Capitol riot and its implications for the nation. They also discussed the challenges of vaccine distribution and the potential consequences of Trump's actions.

notable_moments.description: Discussion of US Capitol riot begins
Democrats' win in Georgia runoff elections is discussed
Friedberg shares his approach to vaccine distribution
